content:
I know there has been a lot of discussion about keeping a branch or branches with WIP rust patches. But why not just keep a registry, at least as a first step?

Just a repo under RFL that has a table in the readme with abstraction, author/contact, status, and link to more. Anyone working on something could add a PR.

This would just give a single place to point anyone to without needing the abstractions to be reviewed or totally ready first
I think thats a good idea!
Figured I may as well start curating something, https://github.com/tgross35/RFL-patch-registry. I'll transfer ownership to the RFL organization if any of the admins are fine with it
@Andreas Hindborg I know you mentioned you had abstraction for htimer and something else - do you have a link?

Trevor Gross said:

Andreas Hindborg I know you mentioned you had abstraction for htimer and something else - do you have a link?

Thanks for getting the ball rolling Trevor. I’ll PR something soonish. The timer stuff is in my null_blk branch.

That is a great idea. Every time I need something I need to search github PR, the RFL mailing list, and zulip to either find it, or see if someone else is working on it.
That would be a huge time saver for me.

Andreas Hindborg said:

Trevor Gross said:

Andreas Hindborg I know you mentioned you had abstraction for htimer and something else - do you have a link?

Thanks for getting the ball rolling Trevor. I’ll PR something soonish. The timer stuff is in my null_blk branch.

Thanks Andreas, anyone can submit PRs or just send links and I'll add them

Looks very useful.
I've submitted abstractions for network drivers ago:
https://lore.kernel.org/netdev/20230613045326.3938283-1-fujita.tomonori@gmail.com/

If we're here sharing abstractions you can have my NAPI abstractions and all of the Ethernet protocols

  • https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1018
  • https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1014

I also think a registry of existing abstractions that people have worked on (with all the appropriate disclaimers), could be very useful. Certainly would have saved me a lot of time looking up GitHub issues (even if in the end I made my own stuff)

Trevor Gross said:

Figured I may as well start curating something, https://github.com/tgross35/RFL-patch-registry. I'll transfer ownership to the RFL organization if any of the admins are fine with it

This is exactly what I wanted to ask ;-) Since you have this POC repo, I think you don't have any problem to maintain it once it's "offical"? ;-) ;-) ;-)
Or maybe we should just put it in the rust-for-linux.com website?
Thanks Amélie and Tomo, added

Boqun Feng said:

Trevor Gross said:

Figured I may as well start curating something, https://github.com/tgross35/RFL-patch-registry. I'll transfer ownership to the RFL organization if any of the admins are fine with it

This is exactly what I wanted to ask ;-) Since you have this POC repo, I think you don't have any problem to maintain it once it's "offical"? ;-) ;-) ;-)

Sure, I can keep doing that :) I assume others will probably submit PRs here and there as they work on stuff

Boqun Feng said:

Or maybe we should just put it in the rust-for-linux.com website?

I didn't realize the website was on github, that seems even better to me if it works
I'll just submit a PR to the website repo once I finish going through the open PRs
I did so here https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/rust-for-linux.com/pull/12. Crazy, almost every area of the kernel has something in progress
@Trevor Gross Any progress on getting your list merged into the RFL website?

Ah, I haven't followed up in a while. When we talked about it last I think the conclusion was that it's preferred if we create a page where patch authors can add themselves, plus wanting to be stricter with what exactly makes it onto the website
I don't exactly remember but I'll try to circle back on that soon
But for now I plan to keep maintaining my list since it's probably more exhaustive than what we have on the website, albeit of course less formal
And I'm starting to grow a section of projects that can be picked up https://github.com/tgross35/RFL-patch-registry#project-idea-board which I'm sure we won't have on the official website
I think we could have the part of the table that maintainers would be OK with in the webpage, i.e. similar to what we discussed for the first table.
